title: Future tense — regular and irregular
objectives: Form the simple future of all regular verbs

vocab.es: hablaré / hablarás / hablará / hablaremos / hablaréis / hablarán

grammar_notes: Take the full infinitive + endings: -é, -ás, -á, -emos, -éis, -án. Same endings for -ar, -er, -ir.
Irregulars change the STEM, keep the endings. Memorize as pairs: tendr-, pondr-, vendr-, saldr-, podr-, sabr-, querr-, har-, dir-.

Spoken Spanish often prefers 'ir a + infinitivo' (the near future) over the simple future. The simple future is also used for PROBABILITY in the present: '¿Dónde está? Estará en casa.' = 'Where is he? He's probably home.'
example_phrases: Tendremos una reunión la próxima semana.
¿Quién será a esta hora? (Who could that be at this time?)
